title: Jackie Brown
logline: A flight attendant caught smuggling money for a gun runner must outwit both the ATF and her criminal employer to escape with her freedom—and a fortune.
antagonistOrConflict: Ordell Robbie (gun runner employer) and ATF agents Dargus and Nicolet, who pressure Jackie from both sides
tone: Cool, methodical, character-driven with bursts of tension and violence; Tarantino's signature blend of laid-back dialogue and sudden danger
themes: The moral compromises of economic desperation

actBreaks.description: Jackie is interrogated by the ATF and realizes she's caught between law enforcement and Ordell, forcing her to devise a survival plan.

validation.ruling: Jackie meets with Nicolet and Dargus and proposes making a deal to help them get Ordell in exchange for keeping her job—this irreversible decision fundamentally shifts the story from her being a victim of circumstance to actively playing both sides, raising stakes and forcing her into new conflict.

actBreaks.description: The mall money exchange goes catastrophically wrong when Melanie is killed and the money disappears, revealing Jackie's true scheme.

validation.ruling: Jackie sits in Nicolet's office after the botched money exchange, claiming Melanie grabbed the bag and was killed for it; Nicolet interrogates her about the missing money while Jackie maintains her story—this scene marks the final major twist where Jackie's deception is complete and the story pivots toward the inevitable confrontation with Ordell.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a patient, character-first crime story that takes its time establishing relationships before executing an intricate con. The first act is unusually long (30 scenes), devoted entirely to world-building: we meet Ordell's operation, watch Beaumont's murder, and only then introduce Jackie and her arrest. This delayed protagonist introduction signals Tarantino's interest in the ecosystem before the individual. Act Two is the longest section, dominated by Jackie's careful manipulation of all parties—she plays the ATF, Ordell, and Max simultaneously, with the mall exchange as the elaborate set piece. The structure mirrors a heist film, but inverted: instead of assembling a team, Jackie isolates each player. The final act is surprisingly brief and intimate, resolving not with action but with character choices about money, freedom, and connection.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately unhurried in the first 30 scenes, establishing Ordell's world through extended dialogue sequences (scenes 3, 5, 27, 29). The rhythm accelerates after Jackie's arrest (scene 30), with shorter, cross-cut scenes building tension. The mall exchange (scenes 67-83) employs rapid intercutting and time stamps to create urgency. Post-climax, the film downshifts again, spending extended time on Jackie and Max's final conversation (scenes 99-100), prioritizing emotional resolution over plot mechanics.

incitingIncident.description: Detective Dargus and ATF Agent Nicolet stop Jackie at LAX, search her bag, find fifty thousand dollars in cash, and arrest her for money laundering.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. LOS ANGELES INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT – DAY

validation.ruling: Jackie is confronted by Detective Dargus and ATF Agent Nicolet at LAX who search her bag and discover she's smuggling money for Ordell, shattering her status quo and forcing her into the central conflict that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: At dinner, Jackie tells Nicolet about Louis Gara working for Ordell; Nicolet reveals Louis just served four years for bank robbery and they've been following him, showing surveillance of Louis and Melanie at the storage facility where they served time together.

validation.ruling: Scene #57 is merely Jackie reporting information to Nicolet over dinner, not a transformative moment. Scene #29 (p46, 31%) is the true midpoint: Ordell confronts Jackie with a gun in her apartment, threatening to kill her if she cooperates with police, but Jackie shifts from reactive victim to proactive strategist by proposing a plan to bring in his half-million while setting up the cops—this is her point of no return where she cannot revert to her original situation and commits to playing both sides.

Ordell forces Max into Cherry Bail Bonds at gunpoint where Jackie and police are waiting in ambush; when Jackie shouts the warning, Dargus shoots and kills Ordell—this is the peak confrontation that resolves the main story question of whether Jackie will survive Ordell's threat and demonstrates her arc completion by successfully orchestrating his downfall.

scenes.heading: INT. LOS ANGELES INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie Brown, a Cabo Air stewardess in her mid-forties, rides a people-mover through LAX airport as opening credits appear.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIRING RANGE – DAY
scenes.summary: A promotional video shows bikini-clad women firing automatic weapons, with Sidney demonstrating a TEC-9.

scenes.heading: INT. MELANIE'S BEAHC APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell Robbie narrates the gun video to Louis Gara and Melanie in her beach apartment, explaining his gun-running business and showing off various weapons on screen.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – DAY
scenes.summary: Ordell and Louis arrive at Cherry Bail Bonds in Ordell's black Mercedes convertible.

scenes.heading: INT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – DAY
scenes.summary: Ordell meets bail bondsman Max Cherry to post bond for Beaumont Livingston, who was arrested for concealed weapons and faces ten years due to probation violation; Ordell pays ten thousand in cash.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – DAY
scenes.summary: Louis uses a remote to unlock Ordell's Mercedes and climbs inside.

scenes.heading: INT. MAX CHERRY'S OFFICE – DAY
scenes.summary: Max informs Ordell that Beaumont faces ten years for violating probation on unregistered machine guns; Ordell provides his address and pays the bond in cash.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAUMONT'S APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beaumont, shirtless and smoking, answers his apartment intercom while watching Jay Leno on TV.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEAUMONT'S APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell arrives at Beaumont's apartment; they embrace and Beaumont thanks him profusely for posting bail; Ordell convinces Beaumont to go for a drive in his car.

scenes.heading: EXT. / INT. OLDSMOBILE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell drives alone in the Oldsmobile listening to R&B, then stops, turns off the music, and retrieves a snub-nosed revolver from the glovebox in darkness.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLDSMOBILE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell opens the trunk where Beaumont is hiding with a shotgun; when Ordell asks if federal agents visited him in jail, Beaumont racks the shotgun and Ordell shoots him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ordell executes Beaumont to prevent him from cooperating with authorities — establishes Ordell's ruthlessness and willingness to kill associates who become liabilities.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis sits in a motel room flipping TV channels and drinking when the phone rings and he answers.

scenes.heading: INT. OLDSMOBILE (PARKED) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell calls Louis from the Oldsmobile, they discuss what Louis is watching on TV, and Ordell tells Louis to come outside because he's parked in front of the motel.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOTEL – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis approaches Ordell's Oldsmobile in the motel parking lot; Ordell tells Louis his problem is thinking he's a good guy and not being willing to take things all the way.

scenes.heading: INT. LOS ANGELES INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT – DAY
scenes.summary: Detective Dargus and ATF Agent Nicolet stop Jackie at LAX, search her bag, find fifty thousand dollars in cash, and arrest her for money laundering.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jackie is arrested with Ordell's money — the inciting incident that forces her into the central conflict between Ordell and the federal agents.

scenes.heading: INT. DARGUS OFFICE – DAY
scenes.summary: In Dargus's office, Nicolet and Dargus interrogate Jackie, offering a cooperation deal; they reveal Beaumont was murdered and suggest Ordell will kill her too if she doesn't help them.

scenes.heading: EXT. TORRANCE MUNICIPAL COURTHOUSE – DAY
scenes.summary: A Los Angeles County Jail bus pulls up behind the Torrance courthouse.

scenes.heading: INT. COUNTY JAIL BUS – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie, wearing county jail blues and handcuffed to another woman, sits on the jail bus as a sheriff explains how they will exit.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTY JAIL BUS – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie and other handcuffed women in county blues exit the bus and are led into the courthouse by sheriffs.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY COURTHOUSE – DAY
scenes.summary: Dargus and Nicolet tell the public defender they'll reduce the charge to possession if Jackie cooperates, or request twenty-five thousand dollar bond if she doesn't.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie tells the public defender to tell the cops they need a better offer; the public defender explains Jackie faces two to four years without cooperation due to her prior record.

scenes.heading: INT. MAX CHERRY'S OFFICE – DAY
scenes.summary: Ordell visits Max Cherry and asks him to post ten thousand dollar bond for Jackie Brown, a stewardess caught with cocaine; Max agrees to transfer Beaumont's bond money to Jackie's case.

scenes.heading: INT. MAX'S CADILLAC (MOVING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Max drives client Anita to county jail, explaining she broke trust with her probation officer by not asking permission to attend her grandmother's funeral.

scenes.heading: INT. L.A. COUNTY JAIL – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Max drops off Anita at the county jail admitting desk and waits on a bench to pick up Jackie Brown.

scenes.heading: INT. MAX'S CADILLAC – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jackie asks Max to prove he's really a bail bondsman by showing ID; she confirms Ordell posted her bond in cash, then asks Max to stop for cigarettes and a drink.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE COCKATOO INN – NIGHT
scenes.summary: A neon cockatoo sign sits atop the red brick Cockatoo Inn.

scenes.heading: INT. THE COCKATOO INN – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jackie and Max drink and talk at the Cockatoo Inn; Jackie explains her situation, reveals the cops want Ordell, and asks Max what he thinks she should do.

scenes.heading: EXT. JACKIE'S APARTMENT COMPLEX – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell watches from his car as Max drops Jackie off at her apartment; after she goes inside and Max drives away, Ordell puts on gloves, takes a pistol, and walks to her apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKIE'S APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell enters Jackie's apartment with a gun, has her make him a drink, and threatens to kill her if she cooperates with police; Jackie proposes a plan to bring in his half-million dollars from Mexico while setting up the cops.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jackie proposes the money exchange scheme to Ordell under threat of death — the plan that will drive the entire second and third acts is conceived and agreed upon.

scenes.heading: INT. LOS ANGELES POLICE DEPARTMENT – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie meets with Dargus and Nicolet and agrees to cooperate; she tells them Ordell has half a million in Mexico and proposes doing a practice run with ten thousand before the real exchange.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKATOO INN
scenes.summary: Jackie calls Ordell from the Cockatoo Inn while Louis and Melanie smoke marijuana at her apartment; Ordell tells Louis to stay and watch cartoons while he goes out.

scenes.heading: INT. MUSIC STORE – DAY
scenes.summary: Max buys a Delfonics CD at a music store after finding the song Jackie played for him.

scenes.heading: INT. THE COCKATOO INN – DAY
scenes.summary: Ordell meets Jackie at the Cockatoo Inn; she explains the exchange plan where she'll hand money to a woman in the food court while cops watch, then switch bags on the second run.

scenes.heading: INT. MELANIE'S BEACH APARTMENT – DAY
scenes.summary: Louis and Melanie get high together; Melanie explains Ordell's gun business and mentions he acts like a big arms dealer but only sells to dopers.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKATOO INN – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie explains to Ordell that she'll do two deliveries — a ten thousand dollar dry run, then the real half-million exchange where she'll switch bags in a dressing room.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: The Del Amo Mall is shown from the parking area with a subtitle identifying it as the largest indoor mall in the world.

scenes.heading: INT. DEL AMO MALL – FOOD COURT – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie and Ordell sit in the Del Amo Mall food court; Jackie explains the exchange procedure where Ordell's girl will take the Broadway bag from under the table.

scenes.heading: INT. DEL AMO MALL – U.A. CINEMAS – DAY
scenes.summary: Max exits a movie theater at the mall and spots Jackie in the food court; they talk and he joins her for coffee; she reveals she knows he's been following her and asks him to help with her plan.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jackie recruits Max into her scheme — he becomes her secret partner against both Ordell and the cops, fundamentally shifting the power dynamics.

scenes.heading: INT. LAX PARKING STRUCTURE – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie arrives at LAX parking structure in her stewardess uniform where Nicolet and Dargus are waiting for her.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKIE'S HONDA – DAY
scenes.summary: The three sit in Jackie's Honda as Nicolet records the trial run on tape and examines the ten thousand dollars Jackie brought from Mexico.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: The huge Del Amo Mall is shown.

scenes.heading: INT. DEL AMO MALL – FOOD COURT – DAY
scenes.summary: Max orders a café mocha at Busta Cap across from the food court, checks his watch, and spots Jackie sitting alone at a table.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKIE'S APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: In a phone conversation, Jackie tells Max to think of Ordell's money as money that shouldn't even be there and questions who has a right to it.

scenes.heading: INT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Max tells Jackie the money is evidence and belongs to the feds, but she argues it's only money right now.

scenes.heading: INT. FOOD COURT – DAY
scenes.summary: A young black woman sits with Jackie in the food court and places her Broadway bag under the table next to Jackie's; Jackie tells her to take Jackie's bag when she leaves.

scenes.heading: INT. DEL AMO MALL – BUSTA CAP – DAY
scenes.summary: Max watches Jackie leave the food court with a Broadway bag; Nicolet and Dargus stop her, examine the bag, talk briefly, then let her go.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEL AMO MALL – PARKING LOT – DAY
scenes.summary: Max follows the young woman to the parking lot and writes down her license plate number as she drives away in a tan Mercury.

scenes.heading: INT. MAX'S CADILLAC – DAY
scenes.summary: Max drives away in his Cadillac, tells himself the plan could work, and plays the Delfonics CD.

scenes.heading: EXT. STORAGE FACLITY – DAY
scenes.summary: Ordell shows Louis his storage facility filled with over half a million dollars worth of guns; they load weapons into a van while Ordell explains his relationships with three women he keeps set up.

scenes.heading: EXT. MELANIE'S APARTMENT BUILDING – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie arrives at Melanie's apartment building and presses the buzzer; Melanie answers through the speaker and Jackie identifies herself.

scenes.heading: INT. MELANIE'S APARTMENT HALLWAY – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie arrives at Melanie's apartment, rings the doorbell, and Melanie opens the door without speaking before walking away; Ordell greets Jackie inside while yelling after Melanie, and Jackie sees Louis sitting on the couch for the first time.

scenes.heading: EXT. MELANIE'S APARTMENT – BALCONY – DAY
scenes.summary: On the balcony, Jackie confronts Ordell about Sheronda passing the money to someone else, which she witnessed despite not being supposed to be there; Ordell defends his right to handle his own money however he wants.

scenes.heading: INT. MELANIE'S APARTMENT – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie explains the plan to Ordell and Louis: Nicolet and Dargus will mark the bills at the airport and follow Sheronda, who doesn't know about the money and thinks she's getting a designer bag; Ordell confirms Sheronda is unaware of the actual exchange.

scenes.heading: INT. MELANIE'S APARTMENT – HALLWAY – DAY
scenes.summary: Walking Jackie to the elevator, Ordell expresses nervousness about her closeness with Ray; Jackie reminds him she'll have to trust him after the delivery, and he reassures her about their partnership while asking if she's told Max about the plan.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE STRAND – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie leaves the apartment building, walks to her car, and spots a beach bar called Sally Leroy's.

scenes.heading: INT. SALLY LEROY'S – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie enters Sally Leroy's to use the phone, waits for a man to finish his call while the camera moves back to reveal Max Cherry sitting at the bar; Max watches Jackie and smiles when she notices him.

scenes.heading: INT. STEAKHOUSE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: At dinner, Jackie tells Nicolet about Louis Gara working for Ordell; Nicolet reveals Louis just served four years for bank robbery and they've been following him, showing surveillance of Louis and Melanie at the storage facility where they served time together.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jackie provides key intelligence about Ordell's operation to the ATF, deepening her cooperation and setting up the players for the exchange.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKIE'S APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Max sits on Jackie's couch drinking wine while Jackie paces with wine and a cigarette, displaying a competitive athlete's edge rather than fear as she reviews tomorrow's plan, explaining that Ordell changed the handoff person from Simone to Melanie.

scenes.heading: INT. MELANIE'S APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell calls someone on the phone to explain that Simone has disappeared with his ten thousand dollars, so Melanie will do the switch instead; Melanie lies on the couch sprawled out in the background.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE BATHROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: In the airplane bathroom, Jackie rearranges her bag, tucking lingerie around the five hundred thousand dollars inside, covering it with blouses, shoes, and skirts, then placing a fat envelope with fifty thousand on top.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN – AIRPLANE – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie exits the bathroom and a passenger complains about waiting for a drink while she spent time in the bathroom; Jackie asks if she called him an asshole, then when he says no, she tells him he's an asshole.

scenes.heading: INT. LAX PARKING STRUCTURE – DAY
scenes.summary: At 3:00 PM in the LAX parking structure, Jackie meets Ray Nicolet who tells her the State Attorney's Office no-filed her case that morning in Circuit Court, meaning she won't go to jail.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jackie learns she's been cleared of charges, removing the legal threat hanging over her and shifting the stakes of the money exchange.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKIE'S HONDA – DAY
scenes.summary: In Jackie's parked Honda, Nicolet opens the flight bag and sees the envelope with fifty thousand on top of clothes; he asks if she checked for cocaine and if she's ever been tempted to steal, then marks the bills with a pen before returning them to the bag.

scenes.heading: INT. MAX CHERRY'S OFFICE – DAY
scenes.summary: At 3:30 PM, Max Cherry puts on his sport coat and tells Winston he's going to Del Amo to see a movie and get something to eat; Winston asks what he'll see and Max says whatever looks best and starts soonest.

scenes.heading: EXT. SAM'S HOFFIN BRAUR – DAY
scenes.summary: At 3:47 PM, Ordell calls from a pay phone at Sam's Hoffin Braur strip club, yelling into the phone asking what the fuck they're still doing there.

scenes.heading: INT. MELANIE'S APARTMENT – DAY
scenes.summary: Louis stands in Melanie's empty living room on the phone with Ordell, who snaps at him to drag Melanie out by the hair because this is his goddamn money; Ordell hangs up on Louis, leaving him pissed at being talked to like that.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEL AMO MALL PARKING LOT – DAY
scenes.summary: At 3:52 PM, Jackie parks at Del Amo Mall, takes a Robinson's/May bag from her hatchback, lines it with paperbacks, pockets one packet of ten thousand from the fifty-thousand envelope, places the remaining forty thousand across the books, fills the rest with beach towels, then heads toward the mall with both bags.

scenes.heading: INT. DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie enters the Del Amo Mall, looks at the people around her knowing any could be surveillance, then calmly walks down the mall and turns into the Robinson's/May store.

scenes.heading: INT. ROBINSON'S/MAY – DESIGNER CLOTHES – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie approaches saleswoman Amy in the designer clothing area about a suit she's had her eye on; Jackie tries on a sharp black suit and Amy compliments how cool and badass she looks in it.

scenes.heading: INT. FITTING ROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie enters the last fitting room stall with her flight bag full of money and the Robinson's/May bag; Melanie enters the adjacent stall and they exchange pleasantries about the black suit through the wall, with Melanie asking if Jackie is getting it.

scenes.heading: INT. DESIGNER CLOTHES – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie, now wearing the black suit but acting frantic and anxious, walks rapidly to the sales counter where Amy waits; she apologizes for staying in the suit to get out of her uniform, completes the transaction while the camera circles them, then mentions someone left a shopping bag with beach towels in the fitting room before leaving.

scenes.heading: INT. DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie walks out of Robinson's/May into the main mall hurriedly, her calm stride completely gone; she stops and looks around frantically with her head darting in all directions while the camera twirls around her, then screams for Ray saying she needs him and that someone took the money; Nicolet, Dargus, and two plainclothes cops come running out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jackie executes her performance of panic, triggering the ATF response and setting the switch in motion — the central con begins to unfold.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT – DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: At 4:12 PM, Louis and Melanie pull up to the Del Amo Mall parking lot; Louis exits first and yells at Melanie to come on because they're late.

scenes.heading: INT. DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: The camera follows Louis pulling Melanie by the hand through the mall, Louis setting a rapid pace; Melanie tells him to get a grip while Louis complains they should have been there already if she hadn't been fucking around, then they enter Robinson's/May.

scenes.heading: INT. ROBINSON'S/MAY – DAY
scenes.summary: The camera follows Louis and Melanie into Robinson's/May where they spot Jackie in her black suit talking to Amy; Melanie comments on Jackie's outfit and says she'll look at a display, but Louis tells her to stay put; when she teases him about sweating and the job being too much, he shoots her a hard convict look, but she smiles and walks away anyway.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT – DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: Louis walks rapidly through the parking lot clutching the shopping bag to his chest with Melanie following; he has a searching look on his face and changes direction between aisles; Melanie laughs and mocks him for not knowing where he parked, asking how he ever robbed a bank.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT – DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: At 4:04 PM, Max Cherry's Cadillac Seville pulls into the Del Amo Mall parking lot and Max gets out, casually strolling towards the mall.

scenes.heading: INT. ROBINSON'S/MAY – DAY
scenes.summary: Max strolls through Robinson's/May with his hands in his pockets, keeping one eye on the Designer Clothes section; he stops at a jewelry counter and tells a salesgirl he's waiting for his wife, then watches Jackie walk out of the fitting room wearing the black suit; he looks back at the jewelry case and says under his breath that they're off.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT – DEL AMO MALL – DAY
scenes.summary: Max walks outside to the parking structure unhurriedly, never looking back; he reaches his blue Seville and uses opening the car door as an excuse to look back at the mall, sees it's normal with nobody after him, allows himself a smile, gets into his Cadillac with his half-a-million bucks and drives away.
scenes.turningPointNote: Max successfully exits with the real money — the switch is complete and Jackie's plan has worked.

scenes.heading: INT. SAM'S HOFFIN BRAUS – DAY
scenes.summary: Ordell sits at the bar in Sam's drinking a screwdriver and watching a stripper when the bartender hands him the phone, warning him not to talk all day.

scenes.heading: INT. TOYOTA (MOVING) – DAY
scenes.summary: Louis drives the Toyota while calling Ordell on his cellular, telling him he got the money but has something else to tell him; Ordell says to tell him in person and asks if he counted it; Louis says it's still in the shopping bag and tries to explain about Melanie giving him a hard time, but Ordell cuts him off and hangs up.

scenes.heading: EXT. SAM'S HOFFIN BRAUR – DAY
scenes.summary: The Toyota pulls up to the back of Sam's Hoffin Braur, Ordell hops in, and the car takes off.

scenes.heading: INT. TOYOTA (MOVING) – DAY
scenes.summary: Ordell grabs the shopping bag from the backseat looking like a kid at Christmas and tells Louis to drive to the car dealerships to swap vehicles; when he asks where Melanie is, Louis explains she bugged him the whole time and ran her mouth, then admits he shot her twice in the parking lot and expects she's dead; Ordell asks why he didn't just talk to her or hit her, and Louis says maybe he could have but at that moment he shot her; Ordell asks if he's sure she's dead and Louis says he didn't go back to check.
scenes.turningPointNote: Louis reveals he killed Melanie, a shocking escalation that changes the stakes and sets Ordell's paranoia in motion.

scenes.heading: INT. NICOLET'S OFFICE (A.T.F. HEADQUARTERS) – DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie sits in Nicolet's office holding coffee and a cigarette while Ray stands; she explains she got there early to try on the suit, planned to change back into her uniform to meet Sheronda, but Melanie came in and grabbed the bag; when Nicolet asks where the other four hundred and fifty thousand is, Jackie says she doesn't know what he's talking about.

scenes.heading: INT. FILTHY APARTMENT – DAY
scenes.summary: Ordell turns to a glassy-eyed junkie named Raynelle nodding on the couch in her filthy apartment and complains about being hung up on, calling the person an ingrate; he comments on how repugnant her apartment is while dialing another number and asking for Max Cherry.

scenes.heading: INT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – DAY
scenes.summary: Winston answers the phone at Cherry Bail Bonds and tells the caller that Max isn't there but is around; when asked for Max's home number, Winston offers his beeper instead.

scenes.heading: INT. FILTHY APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ordell and Raynelle sit on the couch watching Def Comedy Jam without laughing when the phone rings and Ordell jumps on it.

scenes.heading: INT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Max calls Ordell from his office with Winston listening; Max says he has Ordell's ten thousand bond refund and offers to bring it to him; Ordell doesn't respond at first, then after Max reminds him about the bond, Ordell says Jackie got off and asks Max to bring the money; Max asks where he is but Ordell says he knows Max helped Jackie and knows what he wants, threatening that Jackie better hand over his money or she's his and Max is too.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOTEL – NIGHT
scenes.summary: A phone rings inside a room at a low-rent motel.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jackie lies on a motel bed in a t-shirt and panties watching TV when she answers the phone.

scenes.heading: INT. MAX'S OFFICE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Max calls Jackie from his office to tell her he knows where Ordell is; Jackie asks how Winston found him when ATF and police couldn't, and Max explains people trust Winston because he's street; Max says he'll bring Ordell the bond refund money and walk out to call the Sheriff's department, but Jackie says Ray wants him and Max replies that Ray will have to wait his turn.

scenes.heading: EXT. FILTHY APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. RAY NICOLET'S APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. FILTHY APARTMENT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. CADILLAC (MOVING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. CHERRY BAIL BONDS – DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)
